Description: Ability to communicate through reading, writing, speaking and listening in the mother tongue and/or in a foreign language.

All dead languages, no longer actively used, originating from various periods in history, such as Latin from Antiquity, Middle English from the Middle Ages, Classical Maya from the Pre-colonial Americas, and Renaissance Italian from the Early Modern Period.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- all languages that are actively used as a mother tongue or lingua franca Latin, Sanskrit, Ancient Greek.

Description: Sorting, arranging, moving, transforming, fabricating and cleaning goods and materials by hand or using handheld tools and equipment. Tending plants, crops and animals.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Specific skills involving interaction with people.

Level 2 preferred term: handling and disposing of waste and hazardous materials
Description: Collecting, storing and disposing of unwanted materials or hazardous, according to established waste management procedures.

Level 2 preferred term: handling and disposing of waste and hazardous materials
Level 3 preferred term: disposing of non-hazardous waste or debris
Description: Collecting and disposing of unwanted non-hazardous materials according to established waste management procedures.
Scope note:
Examples:
- Handle fish harvesting waste - Collect domestic waste.

Level 2 preferred term: handling and disposing of waste and hazardous materials
Level 3 preferred term: handling and disposing of hazardous materials
Description: Handling, collecting, storing and disposing of materials that pose substantial threats to public or personal health or the environment.
Scope note:
Hazardous waste is defined in accordance with the latest EU guidance on the classification of waste: https://europa.eu/!kr89QF Examples:
- Dispose of medical waste - Handle chemical cleaning agents.

Level 2 preferred term: handling and disposing of waste and hazardous materials
Level 3 preferred term: handling and disposing of waste and hazardous materials
Description: Collecting, storing and disposing of unwanted materials or hazardous, according to established waste management procedures.
Performing physical activities to move, load, unload or store objects, position workpieces or equipment for assembly, or to climb structures, by hand or with the aid of equipment.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Specific skills involving lifting and moving people and animals
- Operation of machinery other than hand-held equipment.

Description: Placing workpieces, materials, tools or equipment in the correct position for assembly or further work.
Performing physical activities to move, load, unload or store objects or to climb structures, by hand or with the aid of equipment.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Specific skills involving lifting and moving people and animals
- Operation of machinery other than hand-held equipment.

Level 3 preferred term: loading and unloading goods and, materials
Description: Loading, securing or unloading products, materials, or equipment for transportation, storage or further processing by hand or using hand-held or manually operated equipment.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Operation of machinery other than hand-held equipment Examples:
- Stow cargo
- Secure cargo on ships
- Unload supplies.

Level 3 preferred term: moving or lifting materials, equipment, or supplies
Description: Moving or lifting goods, materials supplies or resources, by hand or with the aid of equipment, using actions such as lifting, pushing, carrying or shovelling.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Operation of machinery other than hand-held equipment Examples:
- Feed concrete mixer
- Transfer luggage
- Lift heavy weights.

Description: Placing goods and materials in storage by hand, or with the aid of hand-held or manually operated equipment.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Operation of machinery other than hand-held equipment - Storing hazardous material Examples:
- Stock shelves
- Store film reels.

Level 2 preferred term: making moulds, casts, models and patterns
Description: Making moulds, casts and two-dimensional patterns or templates and making three-dimensional models of products.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Operation of automated assembly equipment or machines
- Repair of building structures and mechanical, electrical, electronic products and components.

Level 2 preferred term: making moulds, casts, models and patterns
Description: Creating two dimensional patterns or templates for purposes such as cutting and fabricating articles in different shapes and sizes, or placement of items.
Scope note:
Examples:
- Create floor plan template
- Create patterns for garments.

Level 2 preferred term: making moulds, casts, models and patterns
Description: Constructing moulds for the manufacture of objects in various materials, and making casts by filling moulds with liquid materials for solidification.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Operation of automated assembly equipment or machines
- Pouring of concrete for construction of buildings Examples:
- Modify casts for prostheses
- Mould chocolate
- Cast jewellery metal - Prevent casting adhesion.

Level 2 preferred term: making moulds, casts, models and patterns
Level 3 preferred term: making moulds, casts, models and patterns
Description: Making moulds, casts and two-dimensional patterns or templates and making three-dimensional models of products.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Operation of automated assembly equipment or machines
- Repair of building structures and mechanical, electrical, electronic products and components.

Level 2 preferred term: making moulds, casts, models and patterns
Description: Constructing physical three-dimensional models of products.
Scope note:
Excludes:
- Creation of virtual or conceptual models Examples:
- Create set models
- Build jewellery models
- Make architectural mock-ups.
